tasks.title: Setup Database Schema
tasks.description: Create database migrations and models for the attendance system
tasks.details: Create migrations for esbtp_attendance_codes and esbtp_enseignant_presence tables according to the schema in section 6.1. Implement models with proper relationships. Ensure the code field is unique and 6 characters long. Set up proper timestamps and foreign key constraints. Use Laravel's migration system and Eloquent ORM.
tasks.testStrategy: Run migrations on test database and verify schema structure. Test model relationships and constraints with unit tests.

subtasks.title: Create migration for esbtp_attendance_codes table
subtasks.description: Create a database migration for the attendance codes table that will store all possible attendance status codes
subtasks.details: Use Laravel's migration system to create a new migration file for the esbtp_attendance_codes table. The table should include: id (primary key), code (string, unique, 6 chars), description (string), created_at and updated_at timestamps. Ensure the code field has a unique constraint. Run the migration to create the table in the database. Example command: php artisan make:migration create_esbtp_attendance_codes_table

subtasks.title: Create migration for esbtp_enseignant_presence table
subtasks.description: Create a database migration for the teacher presence table that will track attendance records
subtasks.details: Use Laravel's migration system to create a new migration file for the esbtp_enseignant_presence table. The table should include: id (primary key), enseignant_id (foreign key), date (date), attendance_code_id (foreign key referencing esbtp_attendance_codes.id), comments (text, nullable), created_at and updated_at timestamps. Set up proper foreign key constraints with appropriate cascade actions. Run the migration after the attendance_codes table is created. Example command: php artisan make:migration create_esbtp_enseignant_presence_table

subtasks.title: Implement Eloquent models with relationships
subtasks.description: Create Eloquent model classes for both tables with properly defined relationships
subtasks.details: Create two Eloquent model classes: AttendanceCode and EnseignantPresence. In the AttendanceCode model, define the table name, fillable properties, and a hasMany relationship to EnseignantPresence. In the EnseignantPresence model, define the table name, fillable properties, and belongsTo relationships to both AttendanceCode and the existing Enseignant model. Implement any necessary accessors, mutators, or scopes for convenient data access. Ensure proper namespace and directory structure following Laravel conventions.

tasks.title: Implement Code Generation Logic
tasks.description: Develop the backend logic for generating unique 6-character alphanumeric codes
tasks.details: Create ESBTPAttendanceCodeController with methods for code generation. Implement algorithm to generate random 6-character alphanumeric codes with uniqueness check. Set 24-hour expiration time. Store generated codes in esbtp_attendance_codes table with proper expiration timestamp and initial status.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test code generation for uniqueness, correct length, and proper storage. Test expiration logic and ensure no collisions occur.

subtasks.title: Create ESBTPAttendanceCodeController and database table
subtasks.description: Set up the controller class structure and create the database table for storing attendance codes
subtasks.details: Create a new controller class named ESBTPAttendanceCodeController with basic method signatures for code generation, validation, and management. Create the esbtp_attendance_codes database table with columns for: id (primary key), code (varchar, 6 chars), created_at (timestamp), expires_at (timestamp), status (varchar, for tracking if code is active/used/expired), and any other necessary fields like user_id or session_id if needed for tracking usage.

subtasks.title: Implement code generation algorithm with uniqueness check
subtasks.description: Develop the algorithm to generate random 6-character alphanumeric codes and ensure their uniqueness
subtasks.details: Implement a method in ESBTPAttendanceCodeController to generate random 6-character alphanumeric codes (using A-Z, a-z, 0-9 character set). Add logic to check if generated codes already exist in the database to ensure uniqueness. If a collision occurs, regenerate the code. Include a mechanism to prevent infinite loops in rare cases of multiple collisions. The method should return a valid, unique code ready for storage.

subtasks.title: Implement code storage with expiration logic
subtasks.description: Create methods to store generated codes with proper expiration timestamps and status management
subtasks.details: Implement a method to store the generated codes in the esbtp_attendance_codes table. Set the expiration timestamp to 24 hours after creation. Add methods to check code validity based on expiration time and status. Include functionality to update code status when used. Create a scheduled task or mechanism to clean up expired codes or mark them as expired. Add any necessary error handling and logging for the code generation and storage process.

tasks.title: Develop Admin/Secretary Interface
tasks.description: Create the administrative interface for code management and reporting
tasks.details: Implement routes and views for /esbtp/attendance-codes (index), /esbtp/attendance-codes/generate, /esbtp/attendance-codes/report, and /esbtp/attendance-codes/settings. Use Bootstrap for UI components. Create dashboard showing active codes, usage statistics, and management options. Implement role-based access control to restrict settings to admin users only.
tasks.testStrategy: Test all routes for proper access control. Verify UI components render correctly across browsers. Test CRUD operations on codes through the interface.

subtasks.title: Implement basic routes and views with Bootstrap UI
subtasks.description: Set up the core routes, controllers, and view templates for the attendance code management system with Bootstrap styling
subtasks.details: Create the following routes and corresponding controller methods: GET /esbtp/attendance-codes (index), GET /esbtp/attendance-codes/generate, GET /esbtp/attendance-codes/report, and GET /esbtp/attendance-codes/settings. Implement basic view templates for each route using Bootstrap components. Include navigation between views and a consistent layout. Focus on structure and navigation flow without implementing the actual functionality yet. Create placeholder UI elements that will later be connected to real data.

subtasks.title: Implement dashboard functionality and data display
subtasks.description: Create the data models and functionality to display active codes, usage statistics, and management options in the admin dashboard
subtasks.details: Implement the data models and services needed to track attendance codes, their status, and usage. Create the dashboard view showing active codes with their expiration dates, usage statistics (how many times each code has been used), and management options (activate/deactivate codes). Implement the code generation functionality in the /generate route that creates new unique attendance codes. Build the reporting view that displays historical data about code usage with filtering options. Use charts or graphs where appropriate to visualize usage patterns.

subtasks.title: Implement role-based access control and settings management
subtasks.description: Add user role verification and implement the settings management functionality for admin users
subtasks.details: Create middleware to verify user roles and restrict access to the settings page to admin users only. Implement the settings management functionality that allows admins to configure system-wide parameters such as code expiration time, code format, and access permissions. Add form validation for all settings inputs. Create success/error notifications for all admin actions. Implement confirmation dialogs for critical actions like deleting codes or changing system settings. Ensure all routes are properly secured according to the user's role.

tasks.title: Implement Teacher Attendance Marking
tasks.description: Create the interface and logic for teachers to mark attendance using codes
tasks.details: Develop the /esbtp/attendance/mark route and view with form for code entry. Implement validation logic with clear error messages. Capture IP address and device info for security validation. Limit attempts to 3 per code as specified. Record successful attendance in esbtp_enseignant_presence table.
tasks.testStrategy: Test code validation logic, attempt limiting, and proper error messages. Verify attendance records are created correctly. Test with various devices and network conditions.

subtasks.title: Create attendance marking form and route
subtasks.description: Implement the basic form interface and route for teachers to enter attendance codes
subtasks.details: Create the /esbtp/attendance/mark route and corresponding controller method. Design a simple form with a field for code entry, submit button, and basic layout. Include CSRF protection and form validation. The view should be responsive and follow the application's design patterns. Add appropriate route middleware for authentication to ensure only teachers can access this page.

subtasks.title: Implement code validation logic with security features
subtasks.description: Add server-side validation for attendance codes with security tracking
subtasks.details: Enhance the controller to validate submitted codes against the database. Capture and store the user's IP address and device information (user agent) with each submission attempt. Implement the 3-attempt limit per code as specified, with a lockout mechanism after 3 failed attempts. Create clear, user-friendly error messages for invalid codes, exceeded attempts, and other error conditions. Log all validation attempts for security auditing.

subtasks.title: Complete attendance recording functionality
subtasks.description: Finalize the attendance marking process with database recording
subtasks.details: Implement the final step of recording successful attendance in the esbtp_enseignant_presence table. Create the necessary database operations to mark attendance with timestamps. Include the captured security information (IP, device) in the attendance record. Add success feedback to the user interface after successful attendance marking. Implement any necessary cleanup of temporary data or session information after successful recording. Add comprehensive error handling for database operations.

tasks.title: Implement Teacher History View
tasks.description: Create interface for teachers to view their attendance history
tasks.details: Develop the /esbtp/attendance/history route and view. Display paginated list of past attendance records with dates, times, and status. Implement filtering options by date range and status. Use Bootstrap for responsive design.
tasks.testStrategy: Test history display with various data volumes. Verify filtering works correctly. Test pagination and responsive design on different screen sizes.

subtasks.title: Create basic route and view structure for teacher history
subtasks.description: Set up the route handler and basic view template for the teacher attendance history page
subtasks.details: Create the route handler for '/esbtp/attendance/history' in the appropriate router file. Implement a controller method that will handle the request. Create a basic view template using Bootstrap for responsive design with placeholders for the attendance records list, pagination controls, and filter options. The view should include the page layout, header, and navigation elements consistent with the rest of the application.

subtasks.title: Implement attendance records retrieval and display
subtasks.description: Fetch attendance records from the database and display them in a paginated list
subtasks.details: Modify the controller to retrieve attendance records for the logged-in teacher from the database. Implement pagination logic to limit the number of records per page (suggest 10-15 items per page). Display records in a Bootstrap table showing date, time, and status of each attendance record. Each record should clearly indicate whether the teacher was present, absent, or late. Add pagination controls at the bottom of the list using Bootstrap pagination component. Ensure the view correctly handles cases where no records exist.

subtasks.title: Add filtering functionality for attendance records
subtasks.description: Implement date range and status filtering options for the attendance history
subtasks.details: Add a filter form above the attendance records table with date range picker (start date and end date) and status dropdown (All, Present, Absent, Late). Implement the backend logic to filter records based on the selected criteria. Update the controller to accept and process filter parameters from the request. Ensure the pagination works correctly with filtered results. Add a 'Reset Filters' button to clear all filters. Make sure the current filter state is preserved when navigating between pages of results.

tasks.title: Implement Security Features
tasks.description: Add security measures to prevent fraud and unauthorized access
tasks.details: Implement IP address and device fingerprinting validation. Add rate limiting to prevent brute force attacks. Create automatic code expiration after 24 hours. Add logging for security events. Implement the 3-attempt limit per code as specified in section 6.2.
tasks.testStrategy: Perform security testing including brute force attempts, IP spoofing tests, and attempt limit verification. Test code expiration logic.

subtasks.title: Implement IP and Device Fingerprinting Validation
subtasks.description: Create a system to track and validate user connections based on IP address and device fingerprinting to identify suspicious login attempts
subtasks.details: Create a middleware that captures IP addresses and device information (browser type, OS, screen resolution, etc.) during authentication attempts. Store this information in the database with each verification code request. Implement logic to flag suspicious activities like multiple attempts from different IPs or rapid device switching. Add configuration options to set thresholds for suspicious behavior. Create helper functions to extract and normalize device fingerprints across different platforms.

subtasks.title: Add Rate Limiting and 3-Attempt Verification Limit
subtasks.description: Implement rate limiting for API endpoints and enforce the 3-attempt limit per verification code as specified in section 6.2
subtasks.details: Integrate a rate limiting middleware for all authentication endpoints with configurable thresholds (e.g., max 10 requests per minute per IP). Implement the 3-attempt verification limit by tracking failed attempts in the database. After 3 failed attempts for a specific code, invalidate the code and require a new one to be generated. Create appropriate error responses that inform users about rate limits and remaining attempts. Add headers to responses indicating rate limit status (X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset).

subtasks.title: Implement Code Expiration and Security Event Logging
subtasks.description: Create automatic expiration of verification codes after 24 hours and implement comprehensive logging for security events
subtasks.details: Add a timestamp field to verification codes in the database. Implement logic to check code validity based on creation time, automatically expiring codes after 24 hours. Create a security event logging system that records all security-related events (code generation, verification attempts, suspicious activities, rate limit hits) with appropriate detail levels. Implement a purge mechanism to clean up expired codes from the database. Create an admin interface or API endpoint to review security logs with filtering capabilities. Ensure logs contain sufficient information for forensic analysis without exposing sensitive data.

tasks.title: Develop Reporting System
tasks.description: Create comprehensive reporting tools for administrators
tasks.details: Implement advanced reporting features on /esbtp/attendance-codes/report. Create exportable reports in CSV/PDF formats. Add statistical dashboards showing attendance rates, usage patterns, and anomalies. Implement date range filtering and teacher-specific reports.
tasks.testStrategy: Test report generation with various data sets. Verify export functionality works correctly. Test performance with large data volumes.

subtasks.title: Implement Basic Reporting Interface with Date Filtering
subtasks.description: Create the foundational reporting interface at /esbtp/attendance-codes/report with date range filtering capabilities
subtasks.details: Develop the base reporting page with a clean UI that includes date range pickers (start/end date). Implement the backend API endpoints to fetch attendance data based on selected date ranges. Add teacher selection dropdown to filter reports by specific teachers. Ensure the interface is responsive and follows the application's design patterns. This subtask focuses on the core filtering functionality without the actual report generation.

subtasks.title: Add CSV/PDF Export Functionality
subtasks.description: Implement the ability to export filtered attendance data in CSV and PDF formats
subtasks.details: Build on the reporting interface to add export buttons for CSV and PDF formats. Create backend services to format the filtered data appropriately for each export type. For CSV exports, ensure proper column headers and data formatting. For PDF exports, design a clean, branded template with proper pagination and headers/footers. Include summary information at the top of exports. Test exports with various data volumes to ensure performance and formatting consistency.

subtasks.title: Develop Statistical Dashboards and Visualizations
subtasks.description: Create interactive statistical dashboards showing attendance rates, patterns, and anomalies
subtasks.details: Implement visual dashboards using a charting library (e.g., Chart.js, D3.js) to display key attendance metrics. Create components for: 1) Attendance rate charts showing percentages over time, 2) Usage pattern visualizations highlighting peak attendance periods, 3) Anomaly detection highlighting unusual attendance patterns, and 4) Comparative analysis between different teachers or time periods. Add interactive elements allowing administrators to drill down into specific data points. Ensure all visualizations are responsive and accessible, with appropriate loading states and error handling.

tasks.title: Implement System Configuration
tasks.description: Create admin settings interface for system configuration
tasks.details: Develop the /esbtp/attendance-codes/settings interface for administrators. Add configuration options for code expiration time, attempt limits, and notification settings. Implement role-based access control to restrict access to administrators only.
tasks.testStrategy: Test settings persistence and application. Verify access control prevents unauthorized users from accessing settings. Test each configuration option affects system behavior correctly.

subtasks.title: Create Admin Settings Interface Layout
subtasks.description: Develop the UI layout for the system configuration page at /esbtp/attendance-codes/settings
subtasks.details: Create a responsive admin interface with sections for code expiration time, attempt limits, and notification settings. Include form elements like dropdowns for time selection, number inputs for attempt limits, and toggle switches for notification options. Design should follow the application's existing UI patterns and include save/cancel buttons. Ensure the page has proper routing configured in the application.

subtasks.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control
subtasks.description: Add authentication and authorization checks to restrict access to administrators only
subtasks.details: Modify the route configuration to require admin privileges. Implement middleware or guards that verify the user's role before allowing access to the settings page. Create redirect logic for unauthorized users with appropriate error messages. Test with different user roles to ensure only administrators can access the configuration page. Update navigation menus to only show this option to admin users.

subtasks.title: Connect Settings to Backend and Implement Functionality
subtasks.description: Implement the business logic for saving and retrieving configuration settings
subtasks.details: Create API endpoints for fetching current configuration and updating settings. Implement form submission handlers that validate input and send data to the backend. Add success/error notifications for configuration changes. Ensure settings are immediately applied throughout the system when updated. Include confirmation dialogs for changes that might affect system behavior. Add input validation to prevent invalid configurations.

tasks.title: Implement Offline Mode and Fallback Procedures
tasks.description: Create fallback mechanisms for connectivity issues and forgotten codes
tasks.details: Implement offline mode with local storage and synchronization when connectivity is restored. Create secretary interface for handling forgotten code scenarios. Add manual attendance marking capability for administrators with proper audit logging.
tasks.testStrategy: Test offline functionality by simulating network disconnections. Verify data synchronizes correctly when connection is restored. Test manual attendance procedures and verify audit trail.

subtasks.title: Implement Offline Mode with Local Storage
subtasks.description: Create a mechanism to store attendance data locally when internet connectivity is unavailable and synchronize it when connection is restored
subtasks.details: Implement a service that detects network status changes. When offline, store attendance records in IndexedDB or localStorage with timestamps. Create a background sync mechanism that attempts to send stored records to the server when connectivity is restored. Add visual indicators in the UI to show sync status and pending offline records. Include conflict resolution logic for handling records that may have been modified on the server during offline period.

subtasks.title: Create Secretary Interface for Forgotten Codes
subtasks.description: Develop an interface for secretaries to assist users who have forgotten their attendance codes
subtasks.details: Build a secure verification workflow for secretaries to authenticate users who have forgotten their codes. Implement a temporary code generation system with expiration times. Create an interface for secretaries to search for users by name, ID, or other identifiers. Add audit logging for all secretary-assisted code retrievals. Include security measures to prevent abuse, such as rate limiting and notification to the original user when their code is accessed.

subtasks.title: Implement Manual Attendance Marking for Administrators
subtasks.description: Create functionality for administrators to manually mark or modify attendance records with comprehensive audit logging
subtasks.details: Develop an admin interface for searching and selecting users whose attendance needs manual marking. Implement form controls for administrators to specify attendance details including date, time, reason for manual entry, and supporting documentation. Create a comprehensive audit logging system that records all manual attendance actions with administrator identity, timestamp, reason, and before/after states. Add approval workflows for manual attendance changes that require secondary verification for sensitive modifications. Ensure this system works both online and integrates with the offline mode implemented in subtask 1.

tasks.title: Create Documentation and User Guides
tasks.description: Develop comprehensive documentation for all user types
tasks.details: Create admin user guide, teacher user guide, technical documentation, and maintenance guide as specified in section 8.5. Include screenshots, step-by-step instructions, and troubleshooting information. Document API endpoints and database schema. Create installation and deployment guides.
tasks.testStrategy: Review documentation for completeness and accuracy. Conduct user testing with documentation to verify clarity and usefulness. Verify technical documentation matches implemented system.

subtasks.title: Create End-User Documentation
subtasks.description: Develop comprehensive user guides for administrators and teachers
subtasks.details: Create two separate user guides: (1) Admin User Guide covering system configuration, user management, data management, reporting features, and security settings; (2) Teacher User Guide covering classroom management, assignment creation, student tracking, grading, and communication tools. Both guides should include: table of contents, step-by-step instructions with screenshots for each feature, common workflows, troubleshooting sections, and FAQ. Use clear, non-technical language appropriate for the audience. Format as searchable PDFs with proper navigation.

subtasks.title: Develop Technical Documentation
subtasks.description: Create comprehensive technical documentation including API endpoints and database schema
subtasks.details: Develop technical documentation targeting developers and system administrators. Include: (1) Complete API documentation with endpoint descriptions, request/response formats, authentication requirements, and example calls; (2) Database schema documentation with entity-relationship diagrams, table descriptions, field definitions, and indexing information; (3) System architecture overview showing component relationships and data flows. Use standard technical documentation formats with proper code highlighting. Include diagrams created with appropriate modeling tools. Ensure documentation is version-controlled and includes change history.

subtasks.title: Create Installation and Maintenance Guides
subtasks.description: Develop guides for system deployment, installation, and ongoing maintenance
subtasks.details: Create comprehensive guides for system deployment and maintenance: (1) Installation Guide covering system requirements, step-by-step installation process for all environments (development, testing, production), configuration options, and verification steps; (2) Deployment Guide with instructions for different deployment scenarios, environment setup, and migration procedures; (3) Maintenance Guide covering backup procedures, update processes, performance monitoring, troubleshooting common issues, and security maintenance. Include checklists for regular maintenance tasks and emergency procedures. Format as technical documents with clear sections, command-line examples, and configuration snippets.

tasks.title: Implement Navigation Sidebar in App Layout
tasks.description: Add navigation buttons to the sidebar in app.blade.php to provide access to all previously implemented features and views.
tasks.details:
This task involves updating the main application layout file (app.blade.php) to include a comprehensive navigation sidebar that connects all previously implemented functionality. The developer should:

1. Identify all major features and views implemented in previous tasks
2. Create appropriate navigation buttons/links in the sidebar section of app.blade.php
3. Ensure proper routing is in place for each button
4. Implement visual indicators for the currently active page/section
5. Make the sidebar responsive for different screen sizes
6. Consider user permissions - only show buttons for features the current user has access to
7. Add appropriate icons for each navigation item to improve usability
8. Implement proper translation/localization for all navigation labels
9. Ensure the sidebar maintains a consistent style with the rest of the application
10. Consider adding collapsible sections if there are many navigation items

The sidebar should include links to at least: user management, system configuration, offline mode settings, and any other major features implemented in previous tasks.
tasks.testStrategy:
Testing should verify both the functionality and usability of the sidebar implementation:

1. Functional Testing:
   - Verify each button in the sidebar correctly navigates to its intended view
   - Test that the active page indicator works correctly as navigation occurs
   - Confirm that buttons are only visible to users with appropriate permissions
   - Test sidebar behavior on different screen sizes (desktop, tablet, mobile)
   - Verify collapsible sections expand/collapse correctly if implemented

2. UI/UX Testing:
   - Ensure consistent styling with the rest of the application
   - Verify that icons are appropriate and enhance usability
   - Check that all text is properly translated in different languages
   - Confirm the sidebar doesn't obscure important content on smaller screens

3. Cross-browser Testing:
   - Test the sidebar functionality in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge

4. User Acceptance Testing:
   - Have representative users navigate through the application using only the sidebar
   - Collect feedback on intuitiveness and ease of navigation

subtasks.title: Create Basic Sidebar Structure with Core Navigation Items
subtasks.description: Implement the foundational sidebar structure in app.blade.php with core navigation items and basic styling
subtasks.details:
Implementation steps:
1. Examine app.blade.php to identify the appropriate location for the sidebar
2. Create a responsive sidebar container with appropriate CSS classes
3. Implement the basic sidebar layout with header/logo section
4. Add core navigation items (user management, system configuration, offline mode settings)
5. Include appropriate icons for each navigation item using a consistent icon library
6. Implement basic styling for the sidebar that matches the application's design system
7. Add basic hover and active states for navigation items
8. Ensure the sidebar displays correctly on desktop screen sizes

Testing approach:
- Verify the sidebar appears correctly in the application layout
- Check that all core navigation links are present with appropriate icons
- Test basic styling, hover and active states
- Confirm the sidebar displays properly on desktop screens

subtasks.title: Implement Dynamic Navigation Items with Permission Controls
subtasks.description: Enhance the sidebar to dynamically display navigation items based on user permissions and implement active state indicators
subtasks.details:
Implementation steps:
1. Modify the sidebar to use Blade directives for conditional rendering based on user permissions
2. Implement permission checks for each navigation item (e.g., @can directives)
3. Create a mechanism to determine the currently active page/section
4. Add visual indicators (highlighting, different styling) for the active navigation item
5. Implement proper routing for each navigation item, ensuring links point to correct routes
6. Add translation/localization support for all navigation labels using Laravel's localization features
7. Organize navigation items into logical groups if needed

Testing approach:
- Test with different user roles to verify appropriate items are shown/hidden
- Navigate through different sections to confirm active state indicators work correctly
- Verify all links route to the correct destinations
- Test with different language settings to ensure translations work properly

subtasks.title: Implement Responsive Behavior and Advanced Features
subtasks.description: Make the sidebar fully responsive and implement advanced features like collapsible sections and mobile optimization
subtasks.details:
Implementation steps:
1. Implement responsive behavior for the sidebar on different screen sizes
2. Add a toggle mechanism to show/hide the sidebar on mobile devices
3. Implement collapsible sections for grouped navigation items
4. Add subtle animations for expanding/collapsing sections and mobile toggling
5. Optimize touch targets for mobile users
6. Implement a compact mode for the sidebar (icons only with tooltips) for medium-sized screens
7. Add any remaining navigation items for features implemented in previous tasks
8. Perform final styling adjustments to ensure consistency with the application design

Testing approach:
- Test on various screen sizes (mobile, tablet, desktop) to verify responsive behavior
- Verify collapsible sections expand and collapse correctly
- Test the mobile toggle mechanism works properly
- Check that all animations work smoothly
- Verify the sidebar maintains proper styling across all breakpoints
- Confirm all navigation items are accessible on all screen sizes

tasks.title: Implement User Profile Management Interface
tasks.description: Create a user profile management section accessible from the sidebar that allows users to view and edit their personal information, preferences, and account settings.
tasks.details:
Develop a comprehensive user profile management interface with the following components:

1. Create a new ProfileController with methods for displaying and updating user profiles
2. Design a profile view template with sections for:
   - Personal information (name, email, profile picture)
   - Account settings (password change, notification preferences)
   - Application preferences (language, theme, etc.)
   - Security settings (two-factor authentication, session management)
3. Implement form validation for all editable fields
4. Add profile picture upload functionality with image cropping/resizing
5. Create success/error notifications for all profile actions
6. Ensure all profile data is properly saved to the database
7. Add a dedicated 'Profile' button in the sidebar navigation in app.blade.php
8. Implement proper authorization to ensure users can only edit their own profiles
9. Add breadcrumb navigation for easy return to previous sections
10. Ensure the interface is fully responsive for all device sizes
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests:
   - Test ProfileController methods for proper data handling
   - Validate form submission with both valid and invalid data
   - Test image upload functionality with various file types and sizes

2. Integration tests:
   - Verify profile data is correctly saved to and retrieved from the database
   - Test navigation flow between profile sections
   - Ensure authorization prevents accessing other users' profiles

3. UI/UX testing:
   - Verify all profile sections render correctly on different screen sizes
   - Test keyboard navigation and accessibility compliance
   - Ensure form validation messages are clear and helpful

4. User acceptance testing:
   - Have test users complete common profile management tasks
   - Collect feedback on intuitiveness and ease of use
   - Verify sidebar navigation button is clearly visible and properly labeled

5. Edge cases:
   - Test with extremely long input values
   - Verify behavior when uploading invalid file types
   - Test performance with a large number of concurrent profile updates

subtasks.title: Create ProfileController and Basic Profile View Structure
subtasks.description: Implement the ProfileController with basic methods and create the initial profile view template with navigation structure
subtasks.details:
Implementation steps:
1. Create a new ProfileController with methods:
   - index() - to display the profile view
   - update() - to handle profile updates
   - changePassword() - to handle password changes
2. Set up proper routes in web.php for profile management
3. Create a basic profile view template (profile.blade.php) with tabbed navigation for different sections
4. Add the 'Profile' button in the sidebar navigation in app.blade.php
5. Implement breadcrumb navigation
6. Add authorization middleware to ensure users can only access their own profiles
7. Create the basic responsive layout structure for all device sizes

Testing approach:
- Verify routes are properly registered and accessible
- Test authorization by attempting to access other user profiles
- Check responsive design on multiple screen sizes
- Verify sidebar navigation and breadcrumbs work correctly

subtasks.title: Implement Personal Information and Account Settings Sections
subtasks.description: Build the personal information and account settings sections with form validation and database integration
subtasks.details:
Implementation steps:
1. Create form components for personal information section:
   - Name, email fields
   - Profile picture upload with image cropping/resizing functionality
   - Implement client and server-side validation for these fields
2. Create form components for account settings:
   - Password change form with current/new password fields
   - Notification preferences toggles
   - Implement validation for password requirements
3. Update the ProfileController methods to handle form submissions:
   - Validate input data
   - Save changes to the database
   - Handle profile picture uploads and storage
4. Implement success/error notifications for all form actions
5. Create the necessary database migrations if additional fields are needed

Testing approach:
- Test form validation with valid and invalid inputs
- Verify profile picture upload, cropping and storage works correctly
- Test password change functionality
- Confirm all data is properly saved to the database
- Verify notifications appear correctly on success/error

subtasks.title: Implement Application Preferences and Security Settings
subtasks.description: Complete the profile management interface with application preferences and security settings sections
subtasks.details:
Implementation steps:
1. Create form components for application preferences:
   - Language selection dropdown
   - Theme selection options (light/dark/system)
   - Other application-specific preferences
2. Implement security settings section:
   - Two-factor authentication toggle and setup flow
   - Session management with list of active sessions and logout options
   - Account activity log if applicable
3. Update the ProfileController with methods to handle these new settings
4. Implement the necessary service classes for:
   - Applying theme/language changes throughout the application
   - Managing two-factor authentication
   - Tracking and managing user sessions
5. Ensure all preferences are properly saved and applied immediately
6. Add final polish to the UI for consistency across all sections

Testing approach:
- Test language and theme changes are applied correctly
- Verify two-factor authentication setup and login flow
- Test session management by logging out remote sessions
- Perform end-to-end testing of the complete profile management flow
- Verify all sections maintain proper responsive behavior

tasks.title: Integrate All Feature Access Buttons in Sidebar Navigation
tasks.description: Add navigation buttons to the sidebar in app.blade.php to provide access to all previously implemented features and views throughout the application.
tasks.details:
This task involves updating the sidebar navigation in app.blade.php to include buttons/links for all implemented features. The developer should:

1. Review all previously implemented features and identify all views that need sidebar access
2. Organize the sidebar navigation logically, possibly grouping related features
3. Implement proper icons for each navigation item using an appropriate icon library (Font Awesome, Heroicons, etc.)
4. Ensure each button links to the correct route
5. Implement active state styling to highlight the current section
6. Make the sidebar responsive for different screen sizes
7. Implement proper authorization checks to only show buttons for features the current user has access to
8. Consider adding tooltips for icon-only navigation items
9. Ensure consistent styling across all navigation elements
10. Update any existing sidebar code to maintain a cohesive design

The sidebar should include links to at minimum: dashboard, user profile management, and any other major features implemented in previous tasks.
tasks.testStrategy:
Testing should verify both the functionality and user experience of the sidebar navigation:

1. Functional Testing:
   - Verify each button links to the correct route/view
   - Confirm that clicking each navigation item loads the appropriate page
   - Test that authorization rules correctly show/hide navigation items based on user permissions
   - Verify the active state correctly highlights the current section

2. UI/UX Testing:
   - Test the sidebar on multiple screen sizes to ensure responsive behavior
   - Verify that icons and text are properly aligned and visible
   - Check that hover states and transitions work as expected
   - Ensure consistent styling across all navigation elements

3. Accessibility Testing:
   - Verify that all navigation items are keyboard accessible
   - Check that proper ARIA attributes are used for screen readers
   - Test color contrast for visibility

4. Cross-browser Testing:
   - Verify the sidebar displays and functions correctly in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge

subtasks.title: Audit and organize existing features for sidebar navigation
subtasks.description: Review all implemented features, create a navigation structure, and prepare the sidebar organization
subtasks.details: Review all previously implemented features and views that need sidebar access. Create a logical navigation structure by grouping related features. Document each feature with its route, required authorization level, and suggested icon. Organize items into primary navigation and potentially sub-navigation groups. Determine which features should be visible to which user roles. Create a navigation schema document that will guide the implementation of the next subtasks.

subtasks.title: Implement core sidebar navigation structure and styling
subtasks.description: Build the base sidebar component with proper styling, responsiveness, and active state highlighting
subtasks.details: Update app.blade.php to implement the sidebar navigation structure based on the audit from subtask 1. Choose and integrate an appropriate icon library (Font Awesome, Heroicons, etc.). Implement the base styling for the sidebar including proper spacing, font sizes, and colors. Add responsive behavior to ensure the sidebar works on all screen sizes (consider collapsible behavior on mobile). Implement active state styling to highlight the current section. Add CSS transitions for hover and active states. Ensure the sidebar has a consistent look and feel with the rest of the application.

subtasks.title: Implement navigation items with authorization and interactivity
subtasks.description: Add all navigation links with proper routing, authorization checks, and interactive elements
subtasks.details: Populate the sidebar with all navigation items identified in subtask 1. Implement proper routing for each navigation item using Laravel's route() helper. Add authorization checks (using @can directives or similar) to only display navigation items the current user has access to. Implement tooltips for icon-only navigation items to improve usability. Test all links to ensure they direct to the correct routes. Verify that the active state correctly highlights based on the current route. Ensure all required features are accessible: dashboard, user profile management, and all other major features from previous tasks. Add any final polish like subtle hover animations or transition effects.

tasks.title: Verify Database Migrations for All Existing Models
tasks.description: Ensure that every existing model in the application has corresponding database migrations and verify that all migrations are functioning correctly in the application.
tasks.details:
This task involves a comprehensive audit of all models and their database migrations to ensure data integrity and proper application functionality. The developer should:

1. Create an inventory of all existing models in the application
2. For each model, verify that there are appropriate migrations that create and modify the corresponding database tables
3. Check that all table columns defined in the models have corresponding fields in the migrations
4. Ensure that relationships between models (belongsTo, hasMany, etc.) are properly supported by foreign key constraints in the migrations
5. Verify that any model attributes, such as casts, fillable fields, and hidden attributes, align with the database schema
6. Run migrations in a test environment to confirm they execute without errors
7. Check for any models that might be using tables without proper migrations
8. Document any inconsistencies found and create the missing migrations as needed
9. Ensure that the migration naming follows the project's conventions and includes timestamps for proper sequencing
10. Verify that any seeder data is compatible with the current schema
tasks.testStrategy:
Testing should be thorough to ensure database integrity:

1. Create a fresh test database environment
2. Run 'php artisan migrate:fresh' to verify all migrations run successfully from scratch
3. Run 'php artisan migrate:status' to confirm all migrations are applied
4. For each model, write and execute a simple CRUD test to verify database operations work correctly
5. Test model relationships by creating related records and verifying they can be accessed through the relationship methods
6. Use database schema inspection tools (like 'php artisan db:show' or a database client) to compare the actual database schema with what's defined in migrations
7. Test rollbacks using 'php artisan migrate:rollback' to ensure they work properly
8. Verify that any unique constraints, indexes, and foreign keys are properly created
9. Run the application's existing test suite to ensure all database-dependent functionality works correctly
10. Document any issues found and the fixes applied for future reference

subtasks.title: Create Model Inventory and Audit Migration Files
subtasks.description: Create a comprehensive inventory of all models in the application and audit their corresponding migration files for completeness.
subtasks.details:
1. Scan all directories where models are stored (typically app/Models or similar)
2. Create a spreadsheet or document listing each model with columns for: model name, table name, key attributes, relationships
3. For each model, locate all related migration files in the database/migrations directory
4. Document which models have migrations and which are missing them
5. For models with migrations, verify that the migration file names follow project conventions and include proper timestamps
6. Create a report of any models lacking migration files or having improperly named migrations

subtasks.title: Verify Schema Consistency Between Models and Migrations
subtasks.description: Analyze each model and its corresponding migrations to ensure schema consistency, including columns, relationships, and constraints.
subtasks.details:
1. For each model identified in subtask 1, examine its properties (fillable fields, casts, hidden attributes, etc.)
2. Compare each model's attributes with its migration files to ensure all columns are properly defined
3. Verify that relationships (belongsTo, hasMany, etc.) have corresponding foreign key constraints in migrations
4. Check that column types in migrations match the expected types based on model casts
5. Ensure that any model validation rules are consistent with database constraints
6. Document all inconsistencies found between models and their migrations
7. Create a detailed report highlighting schema mismatches that need to be addressed

subtasks.title: Test Migrations and Create Missing/Corrected Migrations
subtasks.description: Run migrations in a test environment to verify functionality and create any missing or corrected migrations based on the audit findings.
subtasks.details:
1. Set up a clean test database environment for migration testing
2. Run all migrations in sequence to verify they execute without errors
3. Compare the resulting database schema with the expected schema based on models
4. Create new migration files for any models missing migrations (identified in subtask 1)
5. Create corrective migrations for any inconsistencies found (identified in subtask 2)
6. Ensure all new migrations follow naming conventions and include proper timestamps
7. Test the new and corrected migrations to verify they resolve the identified issues
8. Update any seeders that might be affected by schema changes
9. Document all changes made and prepare a final report on the migration verification process

tasks.title: Refactor Codebase for Maintainability and Documentation
tasks.description: Improve code quality through refactoring, standardization, and documentation to enhance long-term maintainability of the application.
tasks.details:
This task involves a comprehensive code cleanup and documentation effort across the codebase:

1. Code Refactoring:
   - Apply SOLID principles to existing classes and methods
   - Remove duplicate code by creating reusable components and helper functions
   - Break down complex methods into smaller, more focused functions
   - Standardize naming conventions across the codebase
   - Optimize database queries and reduce N+1 query problems

2. Documentation:
   - Add PHPDoc comments to all classes and methods
   - Create a README.md with setup instructions and architecture overview
   - Document the purpose of each major component and service
   - Add inline comments for complex logic sections

3. Maintenance Scripts:
   - Create utility scripts for common maintenance tasks
   - Implement a database seeder for testing environments
   - Add commands for cache clearing and environment verification
   - Create scripts to check system health and dependencies

4. Technical Debt:
   - Identify and fix code smells using static analysis tools
   - Update deprecated methods and libraries
   - Improve error handling and logging throughout the application
   - Ensure consistent exception handling patterns
tasks.testStrategy:
The refactoring and documentation efforts should be verified through:

1. Code Quality Metrics:
   - Run static analysis tools (PHPStan, PHPCS, etc.) and ensure zero critical issues
   - Measure code complexity metrics before and after (cyclomatic complexity should decrease)
   - Verify test coverage remains at or above previous levels

2. Documentation Verification:
   - Ensure all public methods and classes have appropriate PHPDoc comments
   - Verify README.md contains accurate setup instructions by having a team member follow them
   - Check that all maintenance scripts have usage examples and documentation

3. Functional Testing:
   - Run the full test suite to ensure refactoring didn't break existing functionality
   - Manually test key user flows to verify application behavior remains unchanged
   - Test all maintenance scripts to ensure they perform their intended functions

4. Peer Review:
   - Conduct a code review with at least two other developers
   - Have team members evaluate documentation clarity and completeness
   - Verify naming conventions are consistent and intuitive to other developers

subtasks.title: Implement Code Refactoring and Standardization
subtasks.description: Apply SOLID principles, remove code duplication, and standardize naming conventions across the codebase to improve maintainability and performance.
subtasks.details: Start by analyzing the codebase to identify areas needing refactoring. Focus on: 1) Applying SOLID principles to existing classes by ensuring single responsibility, proper interfaces, and dependency injection; 2) Extracting duplicate code into reusable components and helper functions; 3) Breaking down methods exceeding 20-30 lines into smaller, focused functions; 4) Standardizing naming conventions (camelCase for methods/variables, PascalCase for classes, etc.); 5) Optimizing database queries by adding proper indexes, eager loading relationships to prevent N+1 problems, and using query builders efficiently. Use static analysis tools like PHPStan or Psalm to identify potential issues. Create a coding standards document to guide future development.

subtasks.title: Create Comprehensive Documentation System
subtasks.description: Develop thorough documentation including PHPDoc comments, README files, architecture overviews, and inline comments for complex logic.
subtasks.details: After refactoring, document the improved codebase: 1) Add PHPDoc comments to all classes and methods, including parameter types, return types, and descriptions; 2) Create a detailed README.md with environment setup instructions, architecture overview diagrams, and development workflows; 3) Document each major component and service with its purpose, responsibilities, and relationships to other components; 4) Add inline comments for complex logic sections explaining the 'why' rather than the 'what'; 5) Generate API documentation if applicable using tools like Swagger/OpenAPI. Ensure documentation follows a consistent format and is placed in appropriate locations. Consider implementing a documentation site using tools like VuePress or Docusaurus for larger projects.

subtasks.title: Develop Maintenance Tools and Address Technical Debt
subtasks.description: Create utility scripts for common maintenance tasks and systematically address technical debt throughout the application.
subtasks.details: Building on the refactored and documented codebase: 1) Create utility scripts for common maintenance tasks including database migrations, cache clearing, and environment verification; 2) Implement comprehensive database seeders for testing environments with realistic test data; 3) Add system health check commands that verify dependencies, permissions, and configuration; 4) Address technical debt by updating deprecated methods and libraries identified during refactoring; 5) Improve error handling with consistent exception types, proper logging with context information, and user-friendly error messages; 6) Set up automated code quality checks using tools like PHP_CodeSniffer, PHPMD, or SonarQube to prevent future technical debt. Package these tools in a way that makes them easily accessible to the development team, such as through composer scripts or custom CLI commands.

tasks.title: Implement Role-Based Dashboard with Access Control
tasks.description: Create a customized dashboard interface that displays different content and functionality based on user roles and their authorized access to specific models and controllers.
tasks.details:
This task involves developing a dynamic dashboard system that adapts its content based on user roles and permissions. Implementation steps include:

1. Define a permission matrix that maps user roles to specific models and controllers they can access
2. Create a dashboard controller that retrieves the user's role and corresponding permissions
3. Design a flexible dashboard layout with component-based sections that can be conditionally rendered
4. Implement dashboard widgets/cards for each functional area (e.g., user management, content management, reporting)
5. Add logic to show/hide dashboard elements based on the user's role permissions
6. Create role-specific navigation menus within the dashboard
7. Implement data retrieval methods that respect access control boundaries
8. Add visual indicators showing which features are accessible to the current user
9. Ensure proper error handling when users attempt to access unauthorized features
10. Optimize dashboard loading performance with appropriate data loading strategies

The dashboard should provide a seamless experience where users only see and can interact with the features they have permission to use. Use Laravel's authorization mechanisms (Gates/Policies) to enforce these permissions consistently.
tasks.testStrategy:
Testing should verify both the correct display of dashboard elements and proper enforcement of access controls:

1. Unit tests:
   - Test the permission matrix logic to ensure it correctly maps roles to accessible models/controllers
   - Verify dashboard controller correctly identifies user roles and permissions

2. Feature tests:
   - Create tests for each user role that verify the correct dashboard components are displayed
   - Test that unauthorized components are properly hidden
   - Verify that direct URL access to unauthorized features is blocked

3. Integration tests:
   - Test the complete user journey through the dashboard for each role
   - Verify that data displayed in dashboard widgets is correctly filtered by permissions

4. Manual testing:
   - Log in as different user roles and verify the dashboard displays appropriate content
   - Attempt to access unauthorized features through URL manipulation
   - Verify that dashboard performance is acceptable with realistic data volumes
   - Test the dashboard on different screen sizes to ensure responsive design

5. User acceptance testing:
   - Have representatives from each user role verify that their dashboard contains all necessary functionality
   - Confirm that the dashboard layout and navigation are intuitive for all user types

tasks.title: Test and Refine Permission System Across User Dashboards
tasks.description: Verify and enhance the permission system to ensure each user dashboard correctly displays content and functionality based on user roles and permissions.
tasks.details:
This task involves comprehensive testing and refinement of the permission system across all dashboard interfaces. The developer should:

1. Create a test matrix of all user roles (admin, manager, regular user, etc.) and their expected permissions for each dashboard section
2. Verify that each dashboard correctly shows/hides elements based on user permissions
3. Test navigation between dashboards to ensure users can only access authorized areas
4. Check that CRUD operations respect permission boundaries (users cannot perform actions they don't have permission for)
5. Review error handling when unauthorized access is attempted
6. Ensure permission changes (e.g., when an admin changes a user's role) are immediately reflected in the dashboard experience
7. Verify that the permission system works consistently across different browsers and devices
8. Optimize permission checks to minimize performance impact
9. Implement caching of permission results where appropriate to improve dashboard loading times
10. Document any edge cases or special permission scenarios for future reference
tasks.testStrategy:
Testing should follow these steps:

1. Create test accounts for each user role in the system
2. Develop automated tests that log in as different users and verify correct dashboard content is displayed
3. Implement UI tests that attempt to access unauthorized sections both through navigation and direct URL access
4. Test API endpoints directly to ensure they enforce permissions independently of the UI
5. Perform cross-browser testing on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge
6. Test permission changes: modify a user's permissions and verify dashboard access changes accordingly without requiring logout/login
7. Test concurrent scenarios: what happens when permissions change while a user is actively using the system
8. Verify performance metrics: ensure permission checks don't significantly impact dashboard loading times
9. Document all test cases and results in a comprehensive test report
10. Have another team member perform independent verification using the test accounts

tasks.title: Implement Notification and Announcement System
tasks.description: Develop a comprehensive notification and messaging system that allows for real-time alerts and announcements to be delivered to users based on their roles and preferences.
tasks.details:
This task involves creating a robust notification system with the following components:

1. **Notification Types**:
   - System notifications (application updates, maintenance alerts)
   - User-specific notifications (task assignments, mentions)
   - Announcements (broadcast messages to all users or specific groups)

2. **Database Structure**:
   - Create a notifications table with fields for: id, user_id, type, content, read_status, created_at, expires_at
   - Create an announcements table with fields for: id, title, content, target_audience, priority, created_by, created_at, expires_at

3. **Backend Implementation**:
   - Develop notification creation services that can be triggered by various system events
   - Implement a notification queue for handling high-volume notifications
   - Create RESTful API endpoints for retrieving, marking as read, and deleting notifications
   - Develop announcement creation and management functionality with role-based access control

4. **Frontend Components**:
   - Create a notification bell icon with counter in the main navigation
   - Implement a dropdown notification panel showing recent notifications
   - Develop a full notification center page for viewing all notifications with filtering options
   - Create an announcement banner for displaying high-priority announcements
   - Implement real-time notification updates using WebSockets or polling

5. **User Preferences**:
   - Allow users to configure which types of notifications they want to receive
   - Implement notification delivery channels (in-app, email, etc.)

Ensure the system integrates with the existing role-based permission system to control who can send announcements and which notifications users receive based on their roles.
tasks.testStrategy:
Testing should cover all aspects of the notification and announcement system:

1. **Unit Tests**:
   - Test notification creation services with various parameters
   - Verify notification retrieval logic works correctly with filters
   - Test announcement creation and targeting functionality

2. **Integration Tests**:
   - Verify notifications are properly triggered by system events
   - Test the notification queue under load conditions
   - Ensure announcements are correctly displayed to targeted user groups

3. **UI Tests**:
   - Verify notification counter updates correctly
   - Test notification panel displays correct information
   - Ensure read/unread status toggles properly
   - Verify announcement banners display and dismiss correctly

4. **User Acceptance Testing**:
   - Create test scenarios for different user roles receiving various notifications
   - Verify administrators can create and target announcements correctly
   - Test notification preferences are respected

5. **Performance Testing**:
   - Test system performance with a high volume of notifications
   - Verify real-time updates work efficiently without excessive resource usage

Document all test cases and expected results. Include edge cases such as notification expiration, handling of notifications for inactive users, and proper cleanup of old notifications.

tasks.title: Implement Comprehensive Testing Suite for Application Functionality
tasks.description: Develop and execute a comprehensive testing suite to verify all application features work correctly according to requirements and previous task implementations.
tasks.details:
Create a structured testing framework that covers all aspects of the application developed in previous tasks. This should include:

1. Unit tests for individual components and functions
2. Integration tests for interactions between different modules
3. End-to-end tests simulating user workflows
4. Permission and role-based access testing
5. Notification system testing
6. Dashboard functionality testing for different user roles
7. Performance testing under various load conditions
8. Security testing including authentication and authorization

The testing suite should be automated where possible using appropriate testing frameworks. Document all test cases clearly with expected outcomes. Create a test report template that highlights passed tests, failed tests, and areas requiring improvement. Ensure test coverage for critical paths is at least 85%. Implement continuous integration to run tests automatically when new code is committed.
tasks.testStrategy:
Verification will be done through:

1. Code review of the testing suite to ensure comprehensive coverage
2. Execution of the complete test suite with documentation of results
3. Verification that all critical features have appropriate test coverage:
   - User authentication and authorization
   - Role-based dashboard content display
   - Permission system functionality
   - Notification and announcement delivery
   - Data integrity across all operations
4. Confirmation that automated tests can be run through CI/CD pipeline
5. Review of test reports for clarity and completeness
6. Verification that edge cases and error conditions are properly tested
7. Manual spot-checking of key functionality to confirm test accuracy
8. Performance test results showing acceptable response times under expected load

The task will be considered complete when all tests pass successfully, the testing documentation is comprehensive, and the test coverage meets the required threshold.

tasks.title: Verify Complete Database Migrations for All Models
tasks.description: Ensure that all database models have complete and properly implemented migrations to maintain data integrity and schema consistency.
tasks.details:
This task involves a comprehensive audit of all database models and their corresponding migration files to ensure proper database schema management. The developer should:

1. Create an inventory of all models in the application
2. For each model, verify that migration files exist for all fields and relationships
3. Check for any missing migrations by comparing model definitions with the current database schema
4. Ensure that migrations handle data type changes, new fields, removed fields, and relationship modifications
5. Verify that migrations include appropriate default values or data transformation logic for existing records
6. Check that migration files are in the correct chronological order
7. Look for any potential migration conflicts or dependencies that might cause issues
8. Document any models with incomplete or missing migrations
9. Create any missing migration files using the appropriate migration generation commands
10. Test migrations in a development environment before applying to production

Special attention should be given to models that have been recently modified or that contain complex relationships. The developer should also ensure that any custom migration logic is properly implemented and tested.
tasks.testStrategy:
Testing should follow these steps to ensure complete verification:

1. Create a fresh database instance from scratch and run all migrations to verify they execute without errors
2. Compare the resulting database schema with the expected schema based on model definitions using schema inspection tools
3. Create a test that programmatically compares each model's fields with the corresponding database table columns
4. Perform a reverse migration (rollback) test to ensure migrations can be reversed without data loss
5. Test migration on a copy of production data to ensure data integrity is maintained
6. Verify foreign key constraints and indexes are properly created
7. Create automated tests that validate the schema matches the expected state after migrations
8. Test edge cases such as nullable fields, default values, and unique constraints
9. Document any discrepancies found between models and their database representations
10. Prepare a migration status report showing the state of each model's migrations

The task is considered complete when all models have corresponding migrations that correctly represent their structure in the database schema, and all tests pass successfully.

tasks.title: Implement Department and Laboratory View Models, Controllers, and Sidebar Navigation
tasks.description: Create view models and controllers for Department and Laboratory modules, and integrate them into the application's sidebar navigation in app.blade.php.
tasks.details:
This task involves several components:

1. Create view models for both Department and Laboratory:
   - Implement DepartmentViewModel and LaboratoryViewModel classes
   - Include necessary properties for data binding
   - Implement validation rules and error handling
   - Add any required data transformation methods

2. Develop controllers for both modules:
   - Create DepartmentController and LaboratoryController classes
   - Implement CRUD operations (index, create, store, show, edit, update, destroy)
   - Add proper request validation
   - Implement appropriate response handling and redirects
   - Ensure proper authorization checks are in place

3. Update the sidebar navigation in app.blade.php:
   - Add new menu items for Department and Laboratory
   - Use appropriate icons that match the application's design system
   - Implement proper routing links to the new controllers
   - Ensure the sidebar items are only visible to users with appropriate permissions
   - Maintain consistent styling with existing sidebar elements

4. Create necessary blade views for both modules:
   - index.blade.php (listing view)
   - create.blade.php and edit.blade.php (forms)
   - show.blade.php (details view)

Ensure all components follow the existing application architecture patterns and coding standards.
tasks.testStrategy:
Testing should cover all implemented components:

1. Unit Tests:
   - Test view model validation rules with valid and invalid data
   - Test controller methods return correct responses
   - Verify proper authorization checks in controllers

2. Integration Tests:
   - Test the complete CRUD workflow for both Department and Laboratory
   - Verify database operations work correctly
   - Test relationships between these models and existing models

3. UI/Navigation Tests:
   - Verify sidebar items appear correctly for users with appropriate permissions
   - Confirm sidebar items are hidden for unauthorized users
   - Test that clicking sidebar links navigates to the correct pages
   - Verify breadcrumb navigation works correctly with the new routes

4. Manual Testing:
   - Navigate through all new pages to verify UI consistency
   - Test responsive design on different screen sizes
   - Verify that form validation messages appear correctly
   - Test with different user roles to ensure proper access control

Document any issues found during testing with screenshots and detailed reproduction steps.
